title: Retire conflicting predecessor PR #1424 after explicit reconciliation
goal: Confirm that every safe and still-relevant requirement from PR #1424 is either present in the reviewed successor or deliberately rejected by the canonical node/faden model, then close #1424 as superseded without merging it.

acceptance.assertion: A semantic comparison records every unique behavior in PR #1424 and classifies it as adopted, superseded or rejected with a reason.
acceptance.verifier: head-bound range comparison and reconciliation note

acceptance.assertion: Direct edge/faden editing or deletion from PR #1424 is not merged unless a separate explicit architecture decision changes the canonical derived-projection model.
acceptance.verifier: spec and final successor diff inspection

acceptance.assertion: PR #1424 is closed with a concise reference to the reviewed successor and its non-adopted architectural scope; it is not merged.
acceptance.verifier: GitHub pull-request state and closing comment

observed_state: PR is draft and conflicting with current main.
Core CI and Required merge gate are failing.
The PR exposes direct edge/faden editing and deletion, while PR #1440 deliberately preserves edges as derived projections.
